name: dYdX Decentralized Exchange API
what_it_does: Decentralized perpetual futures exchange with REST, WebSocket, and gRPC API — enabling traders and developers to access on-chain perpetual futures trading on the dYdX Chain (Cosmos-based Layer 1), market data, order management, and portfolio analytics, with cryptographic signing authentication using Cosmos SDK wallets instead of traditional API keys.
use_cases: Executing perpetual futures via dYdX API for decentralized algorithmic derivatives trading
Accessing real-time market data via dYdX API for on-chain perpetuals price feeds
Managing leveraged positions via dYdX API for decentralized trading bot strategies
Streaming orderbook data via dYdX WebSocket for low-latency DeFi market making
Building portfolio analytics via dYdX API for on-chain trading history and PnL tracking
not_for: Spot trading (dYdX is perpetual futures only — no spot markets)
Fiat on/off ramp (decentralized — crypto-only, no bank transfers)
KYC-compliant institutional trading (decentralized — no identity verification, but OFAC screening)
best_when: A developer wants decentralized perpetual futures trading with on-chain settlement, no counterparty risk, and programmatic access via a well-documented API with official SDKs.
avoid_when: You need spot trading, fiat on-ramps, KYC-compliant institutional infrastructure, or prefer centralized exchange reliability.

auth.notes: Cryptographic wallet signing — no traditional API key. Authentication: Cosmos SDK wallet (mnemonic phrase → private key → address). Order signing: each order signed with STARK private key (derived from Cosmos wallet). Indexer REST API at indexer.dydx.trade/v4 (public, no auth for reads). Validator gRPC for order submission. TypeScript and Python client SDKs handle key derivation and signing. v4: dYdX Chain (own Cosmos L1 blockchain) — different from v3 (Ethereum/StarkEx). NO centralized API key system — non-custodial.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Indexer API: free, public; on-chain trading: gas fees in USDC
pricing.paid_tiers: No subscription; trading fees: 0.02-0.05% maker/taker; gas in USDC on dYdX Chain
pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco) but decentralized protocol — offshore foundation (dYdX Foundation). Founded 2017 by Antonio Juliano (ex-Coinbase, ex-Uber). $87M+ funding (a16z, Paradigm, Three Arrows Capital). dYdX v3 (Ethereum/StarkEx) → v4 (dYdX Chain, own Cosmos L1, 2023). Protocol is open-source (Apache 2.0). DYDX token: governance token. No KYC but geo-blocking: US, UK, Canada, and sanctioned countries blocked at interface level (not protocol level). Indexer: public read API for market data. Validator: write path for order submission. SubaccountS: on-chain isolated margin accounts. 35+ perpetual markets (BTC, ETH, SOL, etc.).

performance.rate_limits: Indexer: 175 req/10 sec per IP; WebSocket: 10 subscriptions/connection

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Wallet signing (NOT API key). Cosmos + STARK key derivation. Python/TypeScript SDKs required. Indexer (reads) vs Validator (writes) split. US/UK/CA geo-blocked at interface. v3 vs v4 are completely different (Ethereum vs Cosmos). Perpetuals only.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: On-chain: all transactions are deterministic and verifiable. Order placement: each signed order is unique (nonce embedded). Market data reads idempotent. Blockchain finality provides ultimate idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: NO traditional API key — authentication is cryptographic wallet signing (Cosmos + STARK private keys)
CRITICAL: v3 (Ethereum/StarkEx) and v4 (dYdX Chain/Cosmos) are completely different APIs — ensure correct version
Key derivation: mnemonic → Cosmos private key → STARK private key (use SDK, do not implement manually)
Reads (Indexer REST) vs Writes (Validator gRPC) use different endpoints and protocols
Geo-blocking: US/UK/Canada/sanctioned countries blocked at interface — use with compliance awareness
Perpetuals ONLY — no spot markets, no fiat on/off ramp; need USDC to start trading

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Cryptographic wallet signing — highest auth strength (private key, not shared secret). Non-custodial: funds in smart contract, not exchange. Open-source protocol: auditable. STARK key derivation: complex but secure when using official SDK. Mnemonic phrase is the ultimate secret — must be secured in hardware or secret manager. OFAC compliance: geo-blocking at interface. No centralized API key attack surface. Validator gRPC: on-chain transaction finality. Protocol risk: smart contract bugs, validator set compromise.
